---
title: "nitrix.stats.ordinal"
generated: true
signature-hash: 9c9758d25e8dcbc1
description: "Ordinal regression -- the cumulative-link (proportional-odds) model."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-ordinal-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-ordinal/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.ordinal`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Ordinal regression -- the cumulative-link (proportional-odds) model.

An *ordered* categorical response $y \in \{0, \ldots, K-1\}$ (clinical
stage, a Likert rating) is modelled through $K - 1$ cumulative thresholds
and a **single** linear predictor shared across them (the proportional-odds
assumption):

$$
P(y_i \le k) = F(\theta_k - x_i^{\top} \beta), \quad
\theta_1 < \theta_2 < \cdots < \theta_{K-1}
$$

so $P(y_i = k) = F(\theta_k - \eta_i) - F(\theta_{k-1} - \eta_i)$ with
$\theta_0 = -\infty$, $\theta_K = +\infty$.  $F$ is the
logistic CDF (`link='logit'` -- the proportional-odds model) or the
standard-normal CDF (`link='probit'` -- ordered probit).  $\beta$ carries
**no intercept** (the thresholds play that role).

This is not the scalar single-predictor IRLS (the likelihood couples the
$K - 1$ ordered thresholds with the regression coefficients), so it gets its
own fitter.  The ordered thresholds are kept ordered by an unconstrained
parameterisation $\theta = \theta_1 + \operatorname{cumsum}([0,
\exp(\delta)])$; the (concave) log-likelihood is maximised by a shared damped
Newton scheme with analytic autodiff curvature.  This pathway is cuSOLVER-free.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `Km1` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-ordinal-symbols}
:::
